title: Silky Fresh Chinese Herring Soup
description: A creamy, low‑carb soup featuring tender herring fillets, coconut milk, and a fragrant ginger‑garlic broth that’s gentle enough for kids yet authentic to Chinese flavor.
instructions: Rinse herring fillets under cold water, pat dry, and cut into bite‑size pieces.
In a medium saucepan, heat sesame oil over medium heat; add minced ginger and garlic, sauté until fragrant (~30 seconds).
Pour in 4 cups water and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, stir in coconut milk, light soy sauce, fish sauce, and Chinese cooking wine; let the broth simmer for 3 minutes.
Gently add the herring pieces; cook 3–4 minutes, or until the fish flakes easily.
Add chopped bok choy and stir; cook for an additional minute until the greens wilt.
Taste and adjust seasoning with extra soy sauce or fish sauce if needed.
Ladle soup into bowls, garnish with sliced scallions and optional chili flakes, and serve hot.
